description_long: This is a single-propeller, low-wing monoplane with a streamlined, dark green fuselage and a light gray propeller at the nose. The wings are straight and extend horizontally from the midsection of the fuselage, with no visible winglets or dihedral angles. The tail section features a conventional design with a single vertical stabilizer and horizontal stabilizers. The cockpit is positioned just behind the propeller, enclosed with a clear canopy, and appears to be a single-seat configuration. The landing gear is fixed, with two main wheels under the wings and a smaller tail wheel. The overall appearance suggests a classic, possibly vintage, light aircraft suitable for personal or training use.
description: A dark green, single-propeller, low-wing monoplane with a streamlined body, fixed landing gear, and an enclosed single-seat cockpit.
description_view_0: Oblique top-rear view showing the streamlined fuselage, low-mounted straight wings, and the tail's conventional design.
description_view_1: Direct front view highlighting the propeller, wing span, and the symmetrical placement of the main landing gear.
description_view_2: Oblique top-front view emphasizing the nose-mounted propeller, cockpit canopy, and the smooth transition to the tail.
description_view_3: Top-down view displaying the full wingspan, fuselage shape, and the relative positions of the cockpit and tail surfaces.
attribute_wingspan_configuration: Low-wing monoplane with straight wings, no visible winglets, and no pronounced dihedral or anhedral angles.
attribute_engine_type: Single nose-mounted piston engine with a two-blade propeller.
attribute_tail_design: Conventional tail with a single vertical stabilizer and two horizontal stabilizers.
attribute_landing_gear_type: Fixed taildragger landing gear with two main wheels and a single tail wheel.
attribute_cockpit_placement: Forward-mounted, enclosed single-seat cockpit with a clear canopy.
